# Work Order & Incident Report — DOC1234
**Document type:** Work Order / Incident Narrative (synthetic narrative record)
**Failure:** F1178 — Overstrain Failure
**Equipment:** EQ-CCM-01 — Continuous Casting Machine #1 (Rajendra Steel Plant, Mumbai Unit - Melt Shop)
**Event timestamp:** 2026-03-30 07:05:09  |  **RCA:** RCA1178 (closed 2026-03-31)

## 1. Incident Narrative
At 07:05:09 on 2026-03-30, the Continuous Casting Machine #1 raised a overstrain failure alarm and was taken out of normal operation. Condition monitoring captured air temperature 300.7 K, process temperature 311.9 K, rotational speed 1991 rpm, torque 76.3 Nm and accumulated tool wear 61 minutes at the moment of the trip. The shift crew isolated the equipment under the plant permit-to-work system and raised this work order for investigation and repair.

## 2. Response & Diagnosis
Anita Iyer (Shift A), Shalini Rao (Shift A) attended the equipment and completed the make-safe checks before handover to the investigation team. The root cause analysis RCA1178, performed by Shalini Naidu (equipment engineer, Shift B), concluded: Root cause identified as mechanical overstrain: the product of tool wear and applied torque exceeded the overstrain threshold for the material variant being processed. Worn tooling combined with high torque demand drove the strain beyond safe limits.

## 3. Procedure Review
The investigation reviewed the governing procedure PROC-009 for this asset and recorded a compliance finding: Step 3 of PROC-009 ("Isolate power and apply lockout-tagout, then bleed hydraulic and pneumatic pressure to zero.") — operating-limit verification was not performed prior to the event. This finding is the actionable link between the maintenance routine and the failure mechanism, and it was logged so recurrence checks can compare like-for-like across the fleet.

## 4. Corrective Work Completed
Replaced worn tooling, re-balanced feed and speed set-points, and tightened the tool-wear replacement interval. Parts and consumables used were booked to this work order, and post-repair verification confirmed the equipment returned to its qualified operating envelope before release back to production.

## 5. Closure
Work order closed on 2026-03-31 with the corrective action verified in service. This record is a synthetic narrative generated for the Synapse knowledge graph and is tagged accordingly; it mirrors the structure of plant work-order documentation for retrieval purposes. Linked records: failure F1178, RCA RCA1178, procedure PROC-009, equipment EQ-CCM-01.
